2605.29274.title: Learnable Assessment Skills for LLM-based Automated Scoring: Rubric Construction via Iterative Optimization
2605.29274.paper_summary: LLM-based automated scoring approaches near-human performance, but scaling to new tasks remains bottlenecked by the per-item human configuration of upstream stages such as rubric construction. Human experts bypass this bottleneck through evaluation heuristics developed over extensive practice. We ask whether LLMs can learn similar heuristics directly from scoring experience, and formalize this as the concept of assessment skills: item-independent natural-language procedural knowledge that guides LLMs through specific stages of the scoring workflow. Focusing on rubric construction as a first instantiation, we propose an iterative framework that decomposes a skill into a fixed scaffold and learnable item-agnostic rules, refining the rules through LLM-driven diagnosis of scoring errors and validation-gated selection. The framework requires no expert-written rubric. On all ten ASAP-SAS items, optimized skills substantially improve LLM-based scoring and frequently surpass the dataset-provided expert rubric. Cross-item transfer experiments further reveal that learned skills capture both generalizable and item-specific patterns.
2605.29274.paper_summary_zh: LLM 基礎的自動評分方法接近人類表現，但擴展到新任務仍然受到如評分標準構建等上游階段每項目人類配置的瓶頸。人類專家通過在廣泛實踐中發展的評估啟發式方法來繞過這一瓶頸。我們詢問 LLM 是否能夠直接從評分經驗中學習類似的啟發式，並將其形式化為評估技能的概念：獨立於項目的自然語言程序知識，指導 LLM 通過評分工作流程的特定階段。以評分標準構建作為第一個具體實例，我們提出了一個迭代框架，將技能分解為固定的支架和可學習的項目無關規則，通過 LLM 驅動的評分錯誤診斷和驗證門控選擇來完善這些規則。該框架不需要專家編寫的評分標準。在所有十個 ASAP-SAS 項目中，優化的技能顯著改善了基於 LLM 的評分，並且經常超過數據集提供的專家評分標準。跨項目轉移實驗進一步顯示，學習到的技能捕捉了可泛化和項目特定的模式。
2605.29274.author: Yun Wang et.al.
2605.29274.authors: Yun Wang, Xin Xia, Xuansheng Wu, Xiaoming Zhai, Ninghao Liu
